input:
hash: 6e12dfb9
date: 2026-07-05
subject: fix(pg_bridge): destructor pool self-deadlock — root cause of the ~59% CI hang
body:
GC can run __del__ while the same thread holds the pool's non-reentrant lock.
Destructors now enqueue to a lock-free deque; get_connection drains it.
diffstat:
core/pg_bridge.py | +48 -9
tests/test_pg_bridge_pool_destructor.py | +110 -0

output.title: pg_bridge: destructor no longer touches pool — fixes ~59% CI hang
output.summary: Fixed a self-deadlock where garbage collection ran PgBridge.__del__ while the same thread held the connection pool's non-reentrant lock. Destructors now enqueue the connection on a lock-free deque and get_connection drains it in normal context. Added a dedicated destructor test file.

input:
hash: a1b2c3d4
date: 2026-06-20
subject: chore(ci): drop python 3.10 from pytest matrix
body:
diffstat:
.github/workflows/test.yml | +1 -4

output.title: CI: python 3.10 dropped from pytest matrix
output.summary: Removed python 3.10 from the pytest matrix in the CI workflow. The matrix now runs on the remaining configured versions only.
output.risk_note: Code that only breaks on python 3.10 will no longer be caught by CI.
